message:
test(conformance): improve shape harness registration introspection + add base fixtures for all extension types

Update ext_conformance_shapes tests to use proper ExtensionManager APIs
for registration snapshot extraction instead of workarounds:

- Replace empty tools list (filtered via `filter(|_| false)`) with
  `manager.extension_tool_defs()` which returns actual registered tool
  definitions from the QuickJS runtime
- Replace hardcoded empty providers vec with `manager.extension_providers()`
  which returns real provider registrations

Add 10 minimal base fixture extensions for shape-aware harness testing:
- minimal_tool: single registerTool call
- minimal_command: slash command registration
- minimal_event: event hook subscription
- minimal_provider: provider registration
- minimal_mcp: MCP server registration
- minimal_resources: resource provider
- minimal_template: template extension
- minimal_configuration: configuration-only extension
- minimal_multi: multi-shape extension (tool + command)
- minimal_ui_component: UI overlay/header registration

Each fixture is a minimal TypeScript file (~300-600 bytes) that exercises
exactly one extension shape through the standard lifecycle, enabling
deterministic offline conformance testing per bd-ljzb.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+---
+name: test-skill
+description: A test skill
+---
+I am a skill.

@@ -89,19 +89,14 @@ fn load_and_snapshot(
         }
     })?;
 
-    // Extract registration snapshot
+    // Extract registration snapshot from manager
     let snapshot = RegistrationSnapshot {
-        tools: manager
-            .list_commands()
-            .iter()
-            .filter(|_| false) // tools are in the tool registry, not commands
-            .cloned()
-            .collect(),
+        tools: manager.extension_tool_defs(),
         slash_commands: manager.list_commands(),
         shortcuts: manager.list_shortcuts(),
         flags: manager.list_flags(),
         event_hooks: manager.list_event_hooks(),
-        providers: Vec::new(), // providers are stored differently
+        providers: manager.extension_providers(),
         models: Vec::new(),
         message_renderers: Vec::new(),
     };
